# Distill
> A thinking development tool for building a reflection practice.

## Site
- Name: Distill
- URL: https://distillwise.com
- Description: Distill is a thinking development tool where you write short reflections after consuming content like books, videos, articles, and podcasts. Your reflections resurface at intervals so your thinking compounds over time.

## Core Concepts
- Reflection Practice: The habit of writing your own perspective after consuming content — not a summary, but your response to what you encountered.
- Spaced Resurfacing: Past reflections resurface at timed intervals, reinforcing and connecting your ideas over time.
- Compound Thinking: Small, regular reflections accumulate into a connected web of ideas. Each new reflection builds on previous ones.
- Active Reading: Engaging deliberately with content by capturing your perspective, converting passive consumption into active thinking.

## How It Works
1. Start a session
2. Consume content externally (books, articles, videos, podcasts)
3. Write a short reflection capturing your perspective (2-5 minutes)
4. Your library grows and past reflections resurface at intervals

## Key Pages
- /blog — Articles on active reading, intentional consumption, and building a thinking practice
- /start — Try writing your first reflection before signing up
- /dashboard — Your personal reflection library and streak tracker (requires account)

## What Distill Is NOT
- Not a note-taking app (no folders, tags, or organizational overhead)
- Not an AI summarizer (you write your own thinking; no AI-generated content)
- Not a journal or diary
- Not a highlight manager or web clipper
- Not a content hosting platform

## Pricing
- Free: 3 deep sessions per month
- Pro: Unlimited reflections, spaced resurfacing, streak tracking

## Tech
- Built with Next.js, Convex, and Clerk
- PWA-enabled for mobile use
